public long smallestNumber(long num) {
    if (num == 0) return 0; // optional, but not necessary as code handles it.

    boolean isNegative = num < 0;
    long absNum = isNegative ? -num : num;

    String s = Long.toString(absNum);
    char[] digits = s.toCharArray();

    if (!isNegative) {
        // positive case
        Arrays.sort(digits);
        int firstNonZero = -1;
        for (int i = 0; i < digits.length; i++) {
            if (digits[i] != '0') {
                firstNonZero = i;
                break;
            }
        }
        if (firstNonZero != -1 && firstNonZero != 0) {
            // swap with index 0
            char temp = digits[0];
            digits[0] = digits[firstNonZero];
            digits[firstNonZero] = temp;
        }
    } else {
        // negative case
        Arrays.sort(digits);
        // reverse the array
        int i = 0, j = digits.length - 1;
        while (i < j) {
            char temp = digits[i];
            digits[i] = digits[j];
            digits[j] = temp;
            i++;
            j--;
        }
    }

    String resultStr = new String(digits);
    long result = Long.parseLong(resultStr);

    return isNegative ? -result : result;
}

solution:
```java
import java.util.Arrays;

class Solution {
    public long smallestNumber(long num) {
        if (num == 0) return 0;

        boolean isNegative = num < 0;
        long absNum = isNegative ? -num : num;

        String s = Long.toString(absNum);
        char[] digits = s.toCharArray();

        if (!isNegative) {
            Arrays.sort(digits);
            int firstNonZero = -1;
            for (int i = 0; i < digits.length; i++) {
                if (digits[i] != '0') {
                    firstNonZero = i;
                    break;
                }
            }
            if (firstNonZero != -1 && firstNonZero != 0) {
                char temp = digits[0];
                digits[0] = digits[firstNonZero];
                digits[firstNonZero] = temp;
            }
        } else {
            Arrays.sort(digits);
            int i = 0, j = digits.length - 1;
            while (i < j) {
                char temp = digits[i];
                digits[i] = digits[j];
                digits[j] = temp;
                i++;
                j--;
            }
        }

        String resultStr = new String(digits);
        long result = Long.parseLong(resultStr);

        return isNegative ? -result : result;
    }
}
```

efficiency: HIGH

time complexity: O(n log n)

space complexity: O(n)

energy implications: LOW

explanation: The solution efficiently rearranges the digits by sorting them and handling leading zeros appropriately. For positive numbers, digits are sorted in ascending order and the first non-zero digit is moved to the front. For negative numbers, digits are sorted in descending order to form the largest possible number, which is then negated to achieve the smallest value. This approach ensures optimal performance with minimal energy usage.
